summary: The question of whether $\psi(x,y) = \psi(y,x)$ for all $x,y \in (0,1]$ remains open. The companion paper arXiv:1908.07453 (Hompe, 2019), which extended the results to additional parameter ranges for $\phi$ and $\psi$, was withdrawn in June 2022 and its content incorporated back into arXiv:1902.10878; neither version appears to resolve the symmetry question for $\psi$. No other follow-up addressing this specific open question was found in the literature.

notes: The companion paper arXiv:1908.07453 was withdrawn (June 2022) with its results merged into the current version of arXiv:1902.10878; it extended computations of $\phi$ and $\psi$ for specific parameter values (e.g., $\geq 3/4$, $2/5$, $3/5$) but did not resolve the symmetry question. The published EJC version (2022, v29i2p47) confirms $\phi(x,y)=\phi(y,x)$ as proven but gives no indication that $\psi(x,y)=\psi(y,x)$ has been settled. No external follow-up paper addressing this question was found.
